2603.29520.title: TrafficMoE: Heterogeneity-aware Mixture of Experts for Encrypted Traffic Classification
2603.29520.paper_summary: Encrypted traffic classification is a critical task for network security. While deep learning has advanced this field, the occlusion of payload semantics by encryption severely challenges standard modeling approaches. Most existing frameworks rely on static and homogeneous pipelines that apply uniform parameter sharing and static fusion strategies across all inputs. This one-size-fits-all static design is inherently flawed: by forcing structured headers and randomized payloads into a unified processing pipeline, it inevitably entangles the raw protocol signals with stochastic encryption noise, thereby degrading the fine-grained discriminative features. In this paper, we propose TrafficMoE, a framework that breaks through the bottleneck of static modeling by establishing a Disentangle-Filter-Aggregate (DFA) paradigm. Specifically, to resolve the structural between-components conflict, the architecture disentangles headers and payloads using dual-branch sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE), enabling modality-specific modeling. To mitigate the impact of stochastic noise, an uncertainty-aware filtering mechanism is introduced to quantify reliability and selectively suppress high-variance representations. Finally, to overcome the limitations of static fusion, a routing-guided strategy aggregates cross-modality features dynamically, that adaptively weighs contributions based on traffic context. With this DFA paradigm, TrafficMoE maximizes representational efficiency by focusing solely on the most discriminative traffic features. Extensive experiments on six datasets demonstrate TrafficMoE consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods, validating the necessity of heterogeneity-aware modeling in encrypted traffic analysis. The source code is publicly available at https://github.com/Posuly/TrafficMoE_main.
2603.29520.paper_summary_zh: 加密流量分類是網路安全中的一項關鍵任務。雖然深度學習已經推進了這個領域，但加密對有效載荷語義的遮蔽嚴重挑戰了標準建模方法。大多數現有框架依賴於靜態和同質的管道，對所有輸入應用統一的參數共享和靜態融合策略。這種一刀切的靜態設計本質上是有缺陷的：通過將結構化標頭和隨機有效載荷強行放入統一的處理管道中，它不可避免地將原始協議信號與隨機加密噪聲糾纏在一起，從而降低了細粒度的區分特徵。在本文中，我們提出了TrafficMoE，一個通過建立解耦-過濾-聚合（DFA）範式突破靜態建模瓶頸的框架。具體而言，為了解決組件之間的結構衝突，該架構使用雙分支稀疏專家混合（MoE）來解耦標頭和有效載荷，使得特定模態的建模成為可能。為了減輕隨機噪聲的影響，引入了一種不確定性感知過濾機制，以量化可靠性並選擇性地抑制高方差表示。最後，為了克服靜態融合的局限性，一種路由引導策略動態聚合跨模態特徵，根據流量上下文自適應地權衡貢獻。通過這種DFA範式，TrafficMoE專注於最具區分性的流量特徵，最大化表示效率。在六個數據集上的大量實驗表明，TrafficMoE始終超越最先進的方法，驗證了在加密流量分析中進行異質性感知建模的必要性。源代碼已公開，網址為 https://github.com/Posuly/TrafficMoE_main。
2603.29520.author: Qing He et.al.
2603.29520.authors: Qing He, Xiaowei Fu, Lei Zhang
